traitGlossary.name: Ability Score Increase
traitGlossary.description: Every race increases one or more of a character's ability scores.

traitGlossary.name: Age
traitGlossary.description: The age entry notes the age when a member of the race is considered an adult, as well as the race's expected lifespan.

traitGlossary.name: Alignment
traitGlossary.description: Most races have tendencies toward certain alignments. These are not binding for player characters.

traitGlossary.name: Size
traitGlossary.description: Characters of most races are Medium. Members of a few races are Small, which affects some rules such as wielding heavy weapons.

traitGlossary.name: Speed
traitGlossary.description: Your speed determines how far you can move when traveling and fighting.

traitGlossary.name: Languages
traitGlossary.description: By virtue of your race, your character can speak, read, and write certain languages.

traitGlossary.name: Subraces
traitGlossary.description: Some races have subraces. Members of a subrace have the traits of the parent race in addition to the traits specified for their subrace.

races.name: Dwarf
races.description: Your dwarf character has an assortment of inborn abilities, part and parcel of dwarven nature.

traits.name: Ability Score Increase
traits.description: Your Constitution score increases by 2.

traits.name: Age
traits.description: Dwarves mature at the same rate as humans, but they are considered young until they reach the age of 50. On average, they live about 350 years.

traits.name: Alignment
traits.description: Most dwarves are lawful, believing firmly in the benefits of a well-ordered society. They tend toward good as well.

traits.name: Size
traits.description: Dwarves stand between 4 and 5 feet tall and average about 150 pounds. Your size is Medium.

traits.name: Speed
traits.description: Your base walking speed is 25 feet. Your speed is not reduced by wearing heavy armor.

traits.name: Darkvision
traits.description: Accustomed to life underground, you have superior vision in dark and dim conditions. You can see in dim light within 60 feet as if it were bright light, and in darkness as if it were dim light. You cannot discern color in darkness, only shades of gray.

traits.name: Dwarven Resilience
traits.description: You have advantage on saving throws against poison, and you have resistance against poison damage.

traits.name: Dwarven Combat Training
traits.description: You have proficiency with the battleaxe, handaxe, light hammer, and warhammer.

traits.name: Tool Proficiency
traits.description: You gain proficiency with the artisan's tools of your choice: smith's tools, brewer's supplies, or mason's tools.

traits.name: Stonecunning
traits.description: Whenever you make an Intelligence (History) check related to the origin of stonework, you are considered proficient in the History skill and add double your proficiency bonus to the check.

traits.name: Languages
traits.description: You can speak, read, and write Common and Dwarvish.

subraces.name: Hill Dwarf
subraces.description: As a hill dwarf, you have keen senses, deep intuition, and remarkable resilience.

traits.name: Ability Score Increase
traits.description: Your Wisdom score increases by 1.

traits.name: Dwarven Toughness
traits.description: Your hit point maximum increases by 1, and it increases by 1 every time you gain a level.

races.name: Elf
races.description: Your elf character has a variety of natural abilities, the result of thousands of years of elven refinement.

traits.name: Ability Score Increase
traits.description: Your Dexterity score increases by 2.

traits.name: Age
traits.description: Although elves reach physical maturity at about the same age as humans, an elf typically claims adulthood and an adult name around the age of 100 and can live to be 750 years old.

traits.name: Alignment
traits.description: Elves love freedom, variety, and self-expression, so they lean strongly toward the gentler aspects of chaos. They value and protect others' freedom as well as their own.

traits.name: Size
traits.description: Elves range from under 5 to over 6 feet tall and have slender builds. Your size is Medium.

traits.name: Speed
traits.description: Your base walking speed is 30 feet.

traits.name: Darkvision
traits.description: Accustomed to twilit forests and the night sky, you have superior vision in dark and dim conditions. You can see in dim light within 60 feet as if it were bright light, and in darkness as if it were dim light. You cannot discern color in darkness, only shades of gray.

traits.name: Keen Senses
traits.description: You have proficiency in the Perception skill.

traits.name: Fey Ancestry
traits.description: You have advantage on saving throws against being charmed, and magic cannot put you to sleep.

traits.name: Trance
traits.description: Elves do not need to sleep. Instead, they meditate deeply, remaining semiconscious, for 4 hours a day. After resting in this way, you gain the same benefit that a human does from 8 hours of sleep.

traits.name: Languages
traits.description: You can speak, read, and write Common and Elvish.

subraces.name: High Elf
subraces.description: As a high elf, you have a keen mind and a mastery of at least the basics of magic.

traits.name: Ability Score Increase
traits.description: Your Intelligence score increases by 1.

traits.name: Elf Weapon Training
traits.description: You have proficiency with the longsword, shortsword, shortbow, and longbow.

traits.name: Cantrip
traits.description: You know one cantrip of your choice from the wizard spell list. Intelligence is your spellcasting ability for it.

traits.name: Extra Language
traits.description: You can speak, read, and write one extra language of your choice.

subraces.name: Wood Elf
subraces.description: As a wood elf, you have keen senses and intuition, and your fleet feet carry you quickly through your native forests.

traits.name: Ability Score Increase
traits.description: Your Wisdom score increases by 1.

traits.name: Elf Weapon Training
traits.description: You have proficiency with the longsword, shortsword, shortbow, and longbow.

traits.name: Fleet of Foot
traits.description: Your base walking speed increases to 35 feet.

traits.name: Mask of the Wild
traits.description: You can attempt to hide even when you are only lightly obscured by foliage, heavy rain, falling snow, mist, and other natural phenomena.

races.name: Halfling
races.description: Your halfling character has a number of traits in common with all other halflings.

traits.name: Ability Score Increase
traits.description: Your Dexterity score increases by 2.

traits.name: Age
traits.description: A halfling reaches adulthood at the age of 20 and generally lives into the middle of their second century.

traits.name: Alignment
traits.description: Most halflings are lawful good. As a rule, they are good-hearted and kind, with no tolerance for oppression.

traits.name: Size
traits.description: Halflings average about 3 feet tall and weigh about 40 pounds. Your size is Small.

traits.name: Speed
traits.description: Your base walking speed is 25 feet.

traits.name: Lucky
traits.description: When you roll a 1 on the d20 for an attack roll, ability check, or saving throw, you can reroll the die and must use the new roll.

traits.name: Brave
traits.description: You have advantage on saving throws against being frightened.

traits.name: Halfling Nimbleness
traits.description: You can move through the space of any creature that is of a size larger than yours.

traits.name: Languages
traits.description: You can speak, read, and write Common and Halfling.

subraces.name: Lightfoot
subraces.description: As a lightfoot halfling, you can easily hide from notice, even using other people as cover.

traits.name: Ability Score Increase
traits.description: Your Charisma score increases by 1.

traits.name: Naturally Stealthy
traits.description: You can attempt to hide even when you are obscured only by a creature that is at least one size larger than you.

races.name: Human
races.description: It is hard to make generalizations about humans, but your human character has these traits.

traits.name: Ability Score Increase
traits.description: Your ability scores each increase by 1.

traits.name: Age
traits.description: Humans reach adulthood in their late teens and live less than a century.

traits.name: Alignment
traits.description: Humans tend toward no particular alignment. The best and the worst are found among them.

traits.name: Size
traits.description: Humans vary widely in height and build, from barely 5 feet to well over 6 feet tall. Your size is Medium.

traits.name: Speed
traits.description: Your base walking speed is 30 feet.

traits.name: Languages
traits.description: You can speak, read, and write Common and one extra language of your choice.

races.name: Dragonborn
races.description: Your draconic heritage manifests in a variety of traits you share with other dragonborn.

traits.name: Ability Score Increase
traits.description: Your Strength score increases by 2, and your Charisma score increases by 1.

traits.name: Age
traits.description: Young dragonborn grow quickly, reach adulthood by 15, and live to be around 80.

traits.name: Alignment
traits.description: Dragonborn tend to extremes, making a conscious choice for one side or the other in the cosmic war between good and evil.

traits.name: Size
traits.description: Dragonborn are taller and heavier than humans, standing well over 6 feet tall and averaging almost 250 pounds. Your size is Medium.

traits.name: Speed
traits.description: Your base walking speed is 30 feet.

traits.name: Draconic Ancestry
traits.description: You have draconic ancestry. Choose one type of dragon from the Draconic Ancestry table. Your breath weapon and damage resistance are determined by the dragon type.

traits.name: Breath Weapon
traits.description: You can use your action to exhale destructive energy. Each creature in the area must make a saving throw determined by your draconic ancestry. The DC equals 8 + your Constitution modifier + your proficiency bonus. A creature takes 2d6 damage on a failed save, and half as much on a successful one. The damage increases to 3d6 at 6th level, 4d6 at 11th level, and 5d6 at 16th level. After you use your breath weapon, you cannot use it again until you complete a short or long rest.

traits.name: Damage Resistance
traits.description: You have resistance to the damage type associated with your draconic ancestry.

traits.name: Languages
traits.description: You can speak, read, and write Common and Draconic.

races.name: Gnome
races.description: Your gnome character has certain characteristics in common with all other gnomes.

traits.name: Ability Score Increase
traits.description: Your Intelligence score increases by 2.

traits.name: Age
traits.description: Gnomes mature at the same rate humans do, and most are expected to settle down into adult life by around age 40. They can live 350 to almost 500 years.

traits.name: Alignment
traits.description: Gnomes are most often good. Those who tend toward law are sages, engineers, researchers, scholars, investigators, or inventors; those who tend toward chaos are minstrels, tricksters, wanderers, or fanciful jewelers.

traits.name: Size
traits.description: Gnomes are between 3 and 4 feet tall and average about 40 pounds. Your size is Small.

traits.name: Speed
traits.description: Your base walking speed is 25 feet.

traits.name: Darkvision
traits.description: Accustomed to life underground, you have superior vision in dark and dim conditions. You can see in dim light within 60 feet as if it were bright light, and in darkness as if it were dim light. You cannot discern color in darkness, only shades of gray.

traits.name: Gnome Cunning
traits.description: You have advantage on all Intelligence, Wisdom, and Charisma saving throws against magic.

traits.name: Languages
traits.description: You can speak, read, and write Common and Gnomish.

subraces.name: Rock Gnome
subraces.description: As a rock gnome, you have a natural inventiveness and hardiness beyond that of other gnomes.

traits.name: Ability Score Increase
traits.description: Your Constitution score increases by 1.

traits.name: Artificer's Lore
traits.description: Whenever you make an Intelligence (History) check related to magic items, alchemical objects, or technological devices, you can add twice your proficiency bonus instead of any proficiency bonus you normally apply.

traits.name: Tinker
traits.description: You have proficiency with artisan's tools (tinker's tools). Using those tools, you can spend 1 hour and 10 gp worth of materials to construct a Tiny clockwork device. The device ceases to function after 24 hours unless repaired, or when you use your action to dismantle it.

options.name: Clockwork Toy
options.description: This toy is a clockwork animal, monster, or person. When placed on the ground, the toy moves 5 feet across the ground on each of your turns in a random direction and makes noises as appropriate.

options.name: Fire Starter
options.description: The device produces a miniature flame, which you can use to light a candle, torch, or campfire. Using the device requires your action.

options.name: Music Box
options.description: When opened, this music box plays a single song at a moderate volume. The box stops playing when it reaches the song's end or when it is closed.

races.name: Half-Elf
races.description: Your half-elf character has some qualities in common with elves and some that are unique to half-elves.

traits.name: Ability Score Increase
traits.description: Your Charisma score increases by 2, and two other ability scores of your choice increase by 1.

traits.name: Age
traits.description: Half-elves mature at the same rate humans do and reach adulthood around the age of 20. They often exceed 180 years.

traits.name: Alignment
traits.description: Half-elves share the chaotic bent of their elven heritage and value both personal freedom and creative expression.

traits.name: Size
traits.description: Half-elves are about the same size as humans, ranging from 5 to 6 feet tall. Your size is Medium.

traits.name: Speed
traits.description: Your base walking speed is 30 feet.

traits.name: Darkvision
traits.description: Thanks to your elf blood, you have superior vision in dark and dim conditions. You can see in dim light within 60 feet as if it were bright light, and in darkness as if it were dim light. You cannot discern color in darkness, only shades of gray.

traits.name: Fey Ancestry
traits.description: You have advantage on saving throws against being charmed, and magic cannot put you to sleep.

traits.name: Skill Versatility
traits.description: You gain proficiency in two skills of your choice.

traits.name: Languages
traits.description: You can speak, read, and write Common, Elvish, and one extra language of your choice.

races.name: Half-Orc
races.description: Your half-orc character has certain traits deriving from your orc ancestry.

traits.name: Ability Score Increase
traits.description: Your Strength score increases by 2, and your Constitution score increases by 1.

traits.name: Age
traits.description: Half-orcs mature a little faster than humans, reaching adulthood around age 14. They age noticeably faster and rarely live longer than 75 years.

traits.name: Alignment
traits.description: Half-orcs inherit a tendency toward chaos from their orc parents and are not strongly inclined toward good.

traits.name: Size
traits.description: Half-orcs are somewhat larger and bulkier than humans, and they range from 5 to well over 6 feet tall. Your size is Medium.

traits.name: Speed
traits.description: Your base walking speed is 30 feet.

traits.name: Darkvision
traits.description: Thanks to your orc blood, you have superior vision in dark and dim conditions. You can see in dim light within 60 feet as if it were bright light, and in darkness as if it were dim light. You cannot discern color in darkness, only shades of gray.

traits.name: Menacing
traits.description: You gain proficiency in the Intimidation skill.

traits.name: Relentless Endurance
traits.description: When you are reduced to 0 hit points but not killed outright, you can drop to 1 hit point instead. You cannot use this feature again until you finish a long rest.

traits.name: Savage Attacks
traits.description: When you score a critical hit with a melee weapon attack, you can roll one of the weapon's damage dice one additional time and add it to the extra damage of the critical hit.

traits.name: Languages
traits.description: You can speak, read, and write Common and Orc.

races.name: Tiefling
races.description: Tieflings share certain racial traits as a result of their infernal descent.

traits.name: Ability Score Increase
traits.description: Your Intelligence score increases by 1, and your Charisma score increases by 2.

traits.name: Age
traits.description: Tieflings mature at the same rate as humans but live a few years longer.

traits.name: Alignment
traits.description: Tieflings might not have an innate tendency toward evil, but many of them end up there. Evil or not, an independent nature inclines many tieflings toward a chaotic alignment.

traits.name: Size
traits.description: Tieflings are about the same size and build as humans. Your size is Medium.

traits.name: Speed
traits.description: Your base walking speed is 30 feet.

traits.name: Darkvision
traits.description: Thanks to your infernal heritage, you have superior vision in dark and dim conditions. You can see in dim light within 60 feet as if it were bright light, and in darkness as if it were dim light. You cannot discern color in darkness, only shades of gray.

traits.name: Hellish Resistance
traits.description: You have resistance to fire damage.

traits.name: Infernal Legacy
traits.description: You know the thaumaturgy cantrip. When you reach 3rd level, you can cast hellish rebuke as a 2nd-level spell once with this trait and regain the ability when you finish a long rest. When you reach 5th level, you can cast darkness once with this trait and regain the ability when you finish a long rest. Charisma is your spellcasting ability for these spells.

traits.name: Languages
traits.description: You can speak, read, and write Common and Infernal.
